hooks.prompt: A Stash-MCP content store is connected. Before performing any content operations in this session, read the deployment-specific usage guide by calling read_content(".stash/SKILL.md"). This document contains conventions, directory structure, templates, and naming patterns specific to this content store. If the file does not exist, proceed without it — the plugin's built-in skill covers universal patterns.

Before starting a new transaction, check for orphaned transactions by calling list_content_transactions() first. If a transaction is already active: (1) if this session owns it, decide whether to commit or abort it before starting a new one; (2) if another session owns it, inform the user that a transaction is in progress and wait rather than proceeding. Do NOT skip this check — orphaned transactions from crashed sessions will block all writes.

You are about to use overwrite_content, which replaces the entire file. Consider whether edit_content would be more appropriate. Use overwrite_content ONLY when: (1) you are regenerating the file entirely from scratch, or (2) the complete new content is intentionally different from the original. If you are modifying part of the file, use edit_content instead — it preserves content you didn't intend to change and is safer when working from partial context. If overwrite_content is genuinely the right choice here, proceed.
